title: Quality management systems — Requirements
structure: High Level Structure (HLS) — aligned with ISO 14001, ISO 45001, ISO 27001

subclauses.name: Understanding the organization and its context
checklist: Identify internal issues relevant to the QMS purpose (e.g. culture, capabilities, processes)
Identify external issues relevant to the QMS purpose (e.g. regulatory, market, competitive)
Monitor and review this information at planned intervals

subclauses.name: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
checklist: Determine relevant interested parties (customers, regulators, suppliers, employees, etc.)
Determine their relevant requirements
Monitor and review this information

subclauses.name: Determining the scope of the QMS
checklist: Consider external/internal issues from 4.1 and requirements from 4.2
Determine applicable products and services
Apply all requirements that can be applied; justify any non-applicable clauses
Make the scope available as documented information

subclauses.name: QMS and its processes
checklist: Determine the processes needed and their application throughout the organization
Determine inputs, outputs, sequence, and interaction of processes
Determine criteria, methods, measures, and KPIs
Assign responsibilities and authorities for processes
Address risks and opportunities for each process
Evaluate and change processes to achieve intended results

subclauses.name: Leadership and commitment
checklist: Top management takes accountability for the QMS
Quality policy and quality objectives are compatible with strategic direction
QMS requirements are integrated into business processes
Process approach and risk-based thinking are promoted
Resources required for the QMS are available
Communicate importance of quality management
QMS achieves its intended results
Support other relevant management roles

subclauses.name: Customer focus (sub-element of 5.1)
checklist: Customer and statutory/regulatory requirements are determined and met
Risks and opportunities that affect product/service conformity are addressed
Customer satisfaction is maintained

subclauses.name: Policy
checklist: Quality policy is appropriate to the organization's purpose and context
Policy provides a framework for quality objectives
Policy commits to satisfying applicable requirements
Policy commits to continual improvement
Policy is available as documented information
Policy is communicated, understood, and applied

subclauses.name: Organizational roles, responsibilities, and authorities
checklist: Responsibilities and authorities are assigned, communicated, and understood
QMS conforms to ISO 9001 requirements
Processes deliver intended outputs
Customer focus is promoted throughout the organization
Integrity of QMS is maintained during changes

subclauses.name: Actions to address risks and opportunities
checklist: Determine risks and opportunities based on 4.1 and 4.2
Plan actions to address risks and opportunities
Integrate actions into QMS processes
Evaluate the effectiveness of these actions
Actions are proportionate to potential impact on conformity

subclauses.name: Quality objectives and planning to achieve them
checklist: Objectives are consistent with quality policy
Objectives are measurable
Objectives take into account applicable requirements
Objectives are relevant to conformity and customer satisfaction
Objectives are monitored and communicated
Objectives are updated as appropriate
Plan: what will be done, resources needed, responsible person, completion date, results evaluation method

subclauses.name: Planning of changes
checklist: Changes to QMS are conducted in a planned manner
Consider purpose of change and potential consequences
Consider QMS integrity, resource availability, responsibility allocation

subclauses.name: Resources
checklist: 7.1.1: Determine and provide resources for QMS establishment, implementation, maintenance, and improvement
7.1.2: Determine competence requirements and ensure persons are competent
7.1.3: Ensure persons are aware of quality policy, objectives, and their contribution
7.1.4: Determine and provide infrastructure needed (buildings, equipment, IT, transport)
7.1.5: Determine and provide the environment for process operation (social, psychological, physical)
7.1.6: Measurement traceability — measuring equipment is calibrated, identified, and protected

subclauses.name: Competence
checklist: Determine competence required for persons affecting QMS performance
Ensure persons are competent (education, training, experience)
Take actions to acquire competence and evaluate effectiveness
Retain documented information as evidence of competence

subclauses.name: Awareness
checklist: Persons are aware of the quality policy
Persons know relevant quality objectives
Persons know their contribution to QMS effectiveness
Persons know the implications of not conforming to QMS requirements

subclauses.name: Communication
checklist: Determine internal and external communications relevant to the QMS
What to communicate, when, to whom, how, and who communicates

subclauses.name: Documented information
checklist: QMS includes all documented information required by the standard and by the organization
Creating and updating: ensure appropriate identification, format, and review/approval
Control: available and suitable for use, adequately protected
Control: distribution, access, retrieval, use, storage, preservation, change control, retention, disposition
Externally originated documented information is identified and controlled

subclauses.name: Operational planning and control
checklist: Plan, implement, control, and maintain the processes needed to meet requirements
Establish criteria for processes and acceptance of products/services
Maintain documented information to have confidence processes were carried out as planned
Control planned changes and review unintended changes
Ensure outsourced processes are controlled

subclauses.name: Requirements for products and services
checklist: 8.2.1: Communicate with customers (information, enquiries, contracts, feedback, complaints)
8.2.2: Determine statutory/regulatory requirements before offering products/services
8.2.3: Review customer requirements and organization ability to meet them before commitment
8.2.4: Amend relevant documented information and inform affected persons when requirements change

subclauses.name: Design and development of products and services
checklist: Establish, implement, and maintain a design and development process
Determine stages, controls, inputs, and outputs
Conduct reviews, verifications, and validations
Control design and development changes; retain documented information

subclauses.name: Control of externally provided processes, products, and services
checklist: Ensure externally provided processes, products, and services conform to requirements
Determine type and extent of controls on external providers
Communicate requirements to external providers
Evaluate, select, monitor, and re-evaluate external providers

subclauses.name: Production and service provision
checklist: 8.5.1: Control conditions for production/service (documentation, monitoring, infrastructure)
8.5.2: Use suitable means to identify outputs and their status throughout production
8.5.3: Preserve customer property; identify, protect, safeguard
8.5.4: Preserve outputs during production and delivery
8.5.5: Meet post-delivery requirements (warranties, maintenance, recycling/disposal)
8.5.6: Review and control changes; retain documented information

subclauses.name: Release of products and services
checklist: Implement planned arrangements to verify requirements have been met
Do not release until all planned arrangements are completed satisfactorily
Retain documented information on release, including person authorizing

subclauses.name: Control of nonconforming outputs
checklist: Identify and control nonconforming outputs to prevent unintended use or delivery
Take appropriate action (correction, segregation, return, suspension)
Obtain authorization for acceptance under concession where applicable
Retain documented information describing the nonconformity and actions taken

subclauses.name: Monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation
checklist: Determine what to monitor and measure, methods, when to analyse and evaluate results
9.1.2: Monitor information relating to customer perception and satisfaction
9.1.3: Analyse and evaluate data to evaluate QMS performance and effectiveness

subclauses.name: Internal audit
checklist: Conduct internal audits at planned intervals
Audit programme: frequency, methods, responsibilities, planning requirements, reporting
Auditors do not audit their own work (objectivity and impartiality)
Report audit results to relevant management
Take appropriate corrections and corrective actions
Retain documented information as evidence of audit programme and audit results

subclauses.name: Management review
checklist: Top management reviews the QMS at planned intervals
Inputs: status of previous actions, changes in context, QMS performance and effectiveness
Inputs: resource adequacy, effectiveness of actions taken on risks and opportunities
Outputs: decisions on opportunities for improvement, changes to QMS, resource needs
Retain documented information as evidence of management review results

subclauses.name: General
checklist: Determine and select opportunities for improvement
Implement actions to meet customer requirements and enhance satisfaction

subclauses.name: Nonconformity and corrective action
checklist: React to the nonconformity: control, correct, deal with consequences
Evaluate the need for action to eliminate causes (root cause analysis)
Implement corrective actions and review their effectiveness
Update risks and opportunities and change QMS if necessary
Retain documented information as evidence of the nature of nonconformity and actions taken

subclauses.name: Continual improvement
checklist: Continually improve suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the QMS
Consider results of analysis and evaluation, management review outputs
Use tools such as PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) cycle
